title: celtis australis subsp. caucasica
intro: Celtis australis subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasian hackberry, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Caucasus region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Celtis australis subsp. caucasica include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exert a protective effect on the cardiovascular system. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies for its purported therapeutic benefits.
